identificationModule.officialTitle: Dexmedetomidine vs Ketamine as Adjuvants to Bupivacaine in Transversus Abdominus Plane Block in Inguinal Hernioplasty: A Randomized Clincal Controlled Trial

interventions.name: Dexmedetomidine & Bupivacaine.
interventions.description: Group A (Dexmedetomidine Group): patients will receive 20 ml solution containing 1 mg/kg bupivacaine + 1mcg/kg dexmedetomidine +normal saline.

interventions.name: ketamine and bupivacaine injection
interventions.description: Group B (ketamine Group): patients will receive 20 ml solution containing 1 mg/kg bupivacaine+0.5 mg/kg ketamine + normal saline.

interventions.name: Bupivacaine
interventions.description: Group C (Control Group): Patients will receive 20 ml solution containing 1 mg/kg bupivacaine + normal saline.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Numeric Pain Rating Scale
primaryOutcomes.description:
The Numeric Pain Rating Scale (NRS) is an effective method for measuring pain intensity. It consists of an 11-point scale where:

0 represents no pain - 10 represents the worst pain imaginable. Patients are asked to select a whole number that best reflects their pain intensity or average pain level.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Immediately postoperative - 2 hours postoperatively - 4 hours postoperatively - 6 hours postoperatively - 12 hours postoperatively

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patients undergoing unilateral inguinal hernioplasty of ASA I or II.
* Age group: 18-60 years old.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Patient refusal.
* known allergy to any of the used drugs
* ASA III or above
* Coagulation disorders and thrombocytopenia
* Body mass index (BMI) \>30
* Infection at needle insertion site
* Patients undergoing bilateral inguinal hernioplasty
